user_query:
I maintain a local list of sustainable gardening incentives for residents and small community groups. I saved a few example program pages under data/pages, and I have a simple Python script in scripts/extract.py that tries to parse them, but it’s brittle and only picks up “rebate” items. Please do a quick code review and refactor so the extractor is configuration-driven and correctly captures all relevant programs.

Requirements:
- Review scripts/extract.py and refactor it so that no hardcoded constants remain for data directory, allowed program types, default region, or output paths. Load those settings from config/config.yaml.
- Fix the current filtering bug: include programs whose type is any of the allowed types in the config (treat program_type comparison as case-insensitive equality after trimming). The initial config lists only "rebate" but please update the config so the allowed types are ["rebate", "grant", "voucher"].
- Improve file handling so only .html files in the configured data_dir are processed, using safe path joins.
- Extract structured records from the local HTML pages and write two outputs:
  1) workspace/output/programs.json — a JSON array where each item has exactly these fields: program_name, sponsor, program_type, description, region, source_file (source_file is the HTML filename the record came from).
  2) workspace/output/programs.csv — a CSV with the same columns and the same records as the JSON (order does not matter, but the sets must match).
- If a program has no region in the HTML, use default_region from the config as a fallback.
- Do not add external dependencies; keep to the Python standard library.
- Deliver a short review note at workspace/output/CODE_REVIEW.md that lists at least three concrete issues you found in the original script (e.g., hardcoded constants, case-sensitive filter, risky path handling, missing CSV output, missing source_file field), and briefly explains your refactoring decisions.

Inputs are the files in this workspace. Please produce the three deliverables at exactly these paths:
- workspace/output/programs.json
- workspace/output/programs.csv
- workspace/output/CODE_REVIEW.md

I’ll verify that:
- The JSON and CSV exist at the specified paths and contain the same set of records.
- Each record includes the six required fields populated from the HTML or config as specified.
- The refactored code reads settings from config/config.yaml and no longer hardcodes paths or filters in the script.

Run the updated extractor to generate the files using the provided HTML pages. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
